feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Inputs no longer visible in Design Mode
entry.content:
Hello, 
Since today, when testing a Task Automation in SAP Build Studio, the mandatory input variables are no longer appearing in the launcher box. Was a new feature introduced or it's a temporary bug? Technical reference here Input/Output Parameters | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP GUI Automation Failing to Recognize Screen – Possible Impact of Windows Idle Session Timeout
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are currently experiencing an issue with one of our SAP GUI screen-based automations (SAP Build Process Automation / SAP GUI scripting).

The automation is able to:

  • Successfully log into Windows

  • Launch SAP GUI

  • Log into SAP

  • Navigate to the required transaction

  • Read data from SAP

However, after performing a period of background processing (Excel data handling), when the automation attempts to continue interacting with SAP GUI, it fails to recognize the expected screen.

At that moment, we observe the following popup message in SAP GUI:

“Idle timer expired. Session has been idle over its time limit. It will be disconnected in 2 minutes. Press any key now to continue session.”

Important observations:

  • SAP GUI does not immediately close.

  • The SAP Logon window remains open.

  • However, the automation is no longer able to recognize the screen elements.

  • The error typically appears as “Page not recognized” or “Element not found”.

We would like to confirm whether:

  1. A Windows Remote Desktop idle session timeout policy is active.

  2. Any recent security or infrastructure changes could be affecting GUI scripting sessions during periods of inactivity.

The automation is screen-based and depends on a stable SAP GUI session without modal popups or session interruptions.

Could you please confirm if the idle timeout policy (Windows or SAP level) may be impacting SAP GUI scripting and RPA execution?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation, My Processes App not working as intended
entry.content:
I am not sure if the My Processes App from Build Process Automation is functioning correctly in Shared Environments.

I was under the assumption that with this app it is possible for users with the ProcessAutomationParticipant role to look at the status of processes in which they have been involved in, as for example, approvers.

I am not able to view any processes in which I am involved in from shared environments that are not owned by me, unless I give myself the Supervise Authorization which leads to me being able to view all processes from that environment, not just the ones I was involved in.

Is this intended? What is the setup so users are only able to only see the processes in which they have been involved, that are running on a shared environment?

entry.title: Automation around Transports (STMS_IMPORT)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am wondering whether somebody from community has "good" experience with automation transport requests in SAP. I am talking about technical transports requests (STMS), not logistics.

In the past I build a process automation with a form where selected users could enter a transport request. The bot checked that request for some preconditions and imported it then into a SAP system.

In the meantime and with much more experience I doubt whether this scenario is really good for a "bot". The check of the preconditions is really complex, because the request is only allowed to have special objects included and nothing else. I am importing with BAPI. There I only can start the import but don't get a reliable feedback (especially for huge requests). Also there are no BAPI for STMS_QA, so I don't get this list clean.

I also noticed that SAP itself doesn't have any automation around STMS_IMPORT in her portfolio (store). That must also have some reasons because there are over 600 projects included and STMS uses nearly every customer.

In the past we dealt that topic with tickets and a manual import from "IT guys".

What are your thoughts about that issue?

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation (IRPA) compatibility with Windows Server 2025
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently evaluating an upgrade of our bot execution server to Windows Server 2025, and we would like to confirm the compatibility of SAP Build Process Automation (Desktop Agent / IRPA) with this operating system.

Our concern is whether running the bots on Windows Server 2025 could affect:

  1. SAP GUI scripting recognition
  2. Screen-based automation stability
  3. RDP session behavior
  4. Desktop Agent execution
  5. Session locking / auto logoff scenarios

Currently, the robots are running on Windows Server (previous version) without issues, but before upgrading we would like to confirm:

  1. Is Windows Server 2025 officially supported by SAP Build Process Automation Desktop Agent?
  2. Are there any known limitations or required configurations?
  3. Could there be impacts on SAP GUI automation or screen element recognition?

Any official documentation or SAP Notes references would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Using Concur API in Build Proces Automation
entry.content:
Hi, 

in SAP Build Process Automation we're trying to access Concur data via API. 

We tried to run GET Retrieve Users (Identity v4 API) and stated /profile/identity/v4 as Resource Path and received this message: 

"{\"status\":400,\"schemas\":[\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error\",\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\"],\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\":{\"messages\":[{\"code\":\"BAD_REQUEST\"}]},\"detail\":\"Company ID is required\"}"

We're assuming that there's something missing within our destination configuration in our BTP subaccount. 
We used the following settings:

Type: HTTP
Proxy Type: Internet
URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID
Client Secret
Token Service URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com/oauth2/v0/token
Token Service URL Type: Dedicated

Additional Properties
companyID


In Concur we generated an OAuth2 App with the Grants refresh_token, client_credentials and password and selected all available scopes.

Any hints on how to make this API work are greatly appreciated!

Thanks
Franziska

entry.title: Insufficient Privilege while creating agent environment in SAP Build Control Tower
entry.content:
Hello, 

As part of working on this tutorial on step 5, I want to create a new environment. I have assigned all the process automation roles - process automation admin, developer, expert etc. But still not able to create the environment and getting the error below.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-setup-desktop-3-0-agent.html

Can you please help.

Thanks,

Mainak

entry.title: Questions about the file attachment feature in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello,

This is my first post and I’m new to SAP Build Process Automation, so I apologize in advance for any omissions or mistakes.
I’ve just started working with SAP Build Process Automation and I’m trying to figure out how to attach files.

I have two questions:

  1. It seems that using a User Task might allow file attachments, but I’m unable to add a User Task to my process. If you have any ideas about what might be causing this, please let me know.
  2. If there is a simpler way to enable file attachments other than (1), I would appreciate your guidance. At the moment, I have limited ability to use other products such as SAP Build Work Zone.

Thank you in advance for your help.

 

>>>Dear Vennilla00,

Thank you very much for your response. If you happen to know, I would be grateful for your guidance.

I can’t seem to find a way to attach a file in my submit form. If you know the procedure or steps, I would greatly appreciate it if you could let me know.

entry.title: SAP Build process automation to Joule Deployment Fails
entry.content: I am trying to deploy a skill from SAP Build Process Automation to Joule, but the Integration Gateway rejects it with the error: 'Cannot use Joule formation connection, ias spba token missing in request header.' My System Landscape Formation is set up correctly, so I suspect this is a Trust Configuration or IAS user mapping issue preventing the required token from generating. Has anyone successfully resolved this specific authentication block during Joule deployments?

entry.title: 403 Error when calling an on-premise S/4 API from SAP Build Process Automation (SAP Desktop Agent).
entry.content:
We have a BTP Destination configured to point to a customer on prem S/4 system via Cloud Connector.

Our goal is to access S/4 APIs (e.g. API_PRODUCT_SRV) on this system from SAP Build Process Automation via SAP Build Actions.

I tested the action directly and these work fine, but when running the action from within a BPA Automation I get a 403 error.

Both the Action test and the BPA Automation action use the same BTP Destination.

Should this be possible and what are the steps I need to take?

Destination:

Authentication: BasicAuthentication
Proxy Type: OnPremise
URL: http://xxxxxxxxx/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/
Additional Properties:
Key
sap.processautomation.enabled
Value
true
Key
MobileEnabled
Value
true
Key
WebIDEEnabled
Value
true
Key
sap-client
Value
200
Key
WebIDEUsage
Value
odata_abap,dev_abap
Key
sap.build.usage
Value
odata_gen
Key
HTML5.DynamicDestination
Value
true
Key
HTML5.Timeout
Value
600000
Key
AppgyverEnabled
Value
true
 
Key
sap.build.processautomation.enabled
Value
true
Key
sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled
Value
true

entry.title: How to remove/delete form from SAP Build Process Automation Inbox after due date?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working with SAP Build Process Automation and have a requirement related to task/forms in the Inbox.

Once a task reaches its due date, I want to automatically remove or delete the associated form/task from the SAP Build Inbox.

Currently, even after the due date is passed, the form still remains visible in the Inbox.

Could you please help with the following:

  • Is there a way to automatically delete or archive forms after the due date?

  • Can this be handled using workflow configuration, timers, or any automation?

  • Are there any best practices to manage expired tasks in the Inbox?

I have already explored workflow settings but could not find an option for automatic removal.

Any guidance or recommended approach would be helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How do I input an expression using the formula editor in Build Process Automation?
entry.content:
I am trying to create the following process:
① Create a trigger form where text can be entered.
② Add an action group (API - Get) within the automation component.
③ Create an expression editor for the API filter that checks if the input fields (4) from ① match the API fields.


Please provide a formula editor for $filter so that all four fields in the trigger form are equivalent to the fields in API - Get.

Currently, I'm getting an error and can't apply the filter properly.


SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Joule Studio Integration with Mapbox MCP
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on a scenario of integration of Joule studio with Mapbox MCP. We are trying to directly use the hosted environment created for the MCP as per the documentation:

MCP Server | API Docs | Mapbox

We have created destination in BTP for the same as below:

Added the required token as authorization as below:

Additional properties as below:

However, while trying to consume in a Joule Studio, it gives up a error as below:

Can you please advise if there is any issue with regards to configuration from my end which is missing or where the issue lies. Any helpful document with regards to this is appreciated.

Thanks

Tirthankar

entry.title: How to: Use OpenText Core Archive for Document Storage/Archiving from SAP BTP / Build?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are designing a solution on SAP BTP using SAP Build / CAP and SAP Build Process Automation and would like to use OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions as our document storage/archiving platform – without involving an SAP ECC/S/4HANA backend for the document upload.

Scenario:

  • We have a SAP Fiori / CAP front-end application with a file upload (PDF) functionality deployed in Cloud Foundry / BTP.
  • Uploaded PDFs should:
    1. Be stored/archived centrally in OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions.
    2. Be retrievable later in SAP Build Process Automation to show the document (PDF) in an approval form for review/approval.
  • We want to call OpenText directly from BTP, not via ECC/S/4HANA, and then access those documents via APIs from workflows in SAP Build Process Automation.

Questions:

  • OpenText Core Archive APIs from BTP

    • Does OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions expose REST APIs that can be called directly from BTP (CAP/Node/Java) for:
      • Creating (uploading) documents (PDF) with metadata?
      • Retrieving documents (either as binary or via a time-limited URL)?
  • Or is there a more “standard” pattern for using Core Archive as a central document store from BTP without ECC/S/4?
  • Are there any official guidelines or recommendations from SAP/OpenText for this type of BTP ↔ Core Archive integration?
  • Even if examples are for Extended ECM or other OpenText products, any patterns applicable to Core Archive would be helpful.

Any inputs, experiences, example architectures, or links to documentation/blogs are highly appreciated.

Thanks! in Advance.

BR,
Rakesh

entry.title: SBPA Destination Error: Destination not found.
entry.content:
I am following below tutorial from developer.sap.com and getting destination connectivity issue.
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

{error: "Error: Destination not found.↵", message: "Destination not found."}
error: "Error: Destination not found.\n" 
message: "Destination not found."

Steps

1. I have completed Create a Sales Order Process Using SAP Build Process Automation Successfully.

2. After that I have created Create a Sales Order App Using SAP Build Apps from SAP Build Successfully.

3. When I try to access from Build App Preview getting above destination issue.

4. As per tutorial it will work like this.

Regards,

Abderrahmane Kermiche

entry.title: How to manage documents in SAP Document AI
entry.content:
Hi, 

The tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-aibus-dox-swagger-ui.html shows how to upload documents in SAP Document AI via API call. So I have following questions. 

1. If I develop a solution e.g. in SAP Build Process Automation to call SAP Document API, then there will numerous documents that will get uploaded in the document AI to extract information. After an extraction is over, the document is not used again.

Because I think we can't reuse the same document already uploaded instead of user again uploading it. So is there any best practice on how to manage the huge volume of documents after extraction is over. Should we set up another automation to delete the historical documents via calling an API?

2. In this tutorial, we are first creating a client. Is this an one time activity? Is this done to keep my test separate from others. In the productive use case, do I really need to setup client. The Document AI UI doesn't provide the option to client management.(at lease the base plan).

Thanks,
Mainak

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation call form in Browser after Trigger
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

At the beginning of the process, I launch a trigger form via a provided link after deployment to input the path to an invoice. This then starts an automation that extracts the relevant data from the invoice. The extracted data is subsequently passed to an approval form, which is currently displayed in the inbox.

Instead of opening the approval form in the inbox, I would prefer to remain in the browser and be automatically redirected to the form that displays the extracted data from the automation.
Is this possible?

Best Regards,

Carlo

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Notification step fails with "this.certificates is null"
entry.content: Issue resolved, no longer needed

entry.title: Question on Automatic Clearing Rules – Do All Selected Criteria Need to Match?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a question regarding the Automatic Clearing of Open Items configuration in SAP (transaction OB74).

When defining clearing rules, SAP allows us to specify up to five matching criteria. My understanding is that all selected criteria must match in order for automatic clearing to occur.
Can you please confirm if this is correct?

Additionally, we have a business requirement where different combinations of fields should trigger clearing independently, for example:

  • Clear if Amount + Reference (XBLNR) match
  • Clear if Amount + Billing Document (VBLEN) match
  • Clear if Amount + Assignment (ZUONR) match

Is there a way to configure SAP so that each of these combinations works as an independent clearing rule, instead of requiring all fields in the OB74 configuration to match at the same time?

Any guidance on how to set this up—or whether multiple parallel clearing rules are possible—would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: SBPA Desktop Automation -- How to select a specific value from ARIBA DDM dropdown(bh=DDM) at runtime
entry.content:
Environment:

  • SAP Build Process Automation (Cloud Studio)
  • Desktop Agent, ARIBA framework application
  • Browser: Microsoft Edge
  • Target: SAP Ariba Log Settings page

Problem:

I need to programmatically select a specific value from an ARIBA DDM dropdown (bh=DDM, role=combobox) during a bot automation. The value to select changes dynamically per loop iteration (node name from a NodeItem loop variable).

The dropdown contains 342 items and uses virtual rendering -- items are only loaded into the DOM when the dropdown is open and filtered by typing.

What I have declared:

  • e_logicalNode -- the DDM element (DIV bh=DDM role=combobox)
  • e_allDDI -- collection of all DDI items (bh=DDI role=option, 342 elements)

What I have tried:

Attempt 1 -- Click DDM + Keystroke to filter + Click e_allDDI[0]:

  • Click e_logicalNode opens the dropdown
  • Keystroke with expression editor types the node name (confirmed resolving correctly via Log Message)
  • Click e_allDDI[0] always clicks the first item in the full unfiltered collection, not the filtered result

Attempt 2 -- Click DDM + Keystroke + {Return}:

  • Click e_logicalNode opens the dropdown
  • Keystroke {Ctrl}A + {Delete} to clear filter, then type node name, then {Return}
  • Dropdown opens but Keystroke does not interact with the filter -- default node remains selected
  • Root cause suspected: clicking the DDM div opens the popup but focus does NOT land on the filter input inside the popup

Attempt 3 -- Script Element (WEB framework):

  • Created a WEB framework application and used Script Element activity to execute JavaScript
  • All JavaScript approaches fail: document, window, and element are all undefined in the Script Element content context
  • Error: SCRIPTITEM failed in content context: Cannot read properties of undefined

Confirmed facts:

  • DDI items ARE in the DOM even when dropdown is closed (document.querySelector('[awname*=C1_UI1]') found via browser console)
  • mousedown + mouseup + click events on a DDI item via browser console DO select it correctly
  • The filter input inside the DDM popup is a separate element that requires explicit focus before typing

Question:

  1. How do I correctly select a specific value from an ARIBA DDM dropdown at runtime in SBPA Desktop Automation?
  2. Is there a native SBPA activity for ARIBA DDM value selection (similar to SAPUI5 Combo Box Set Value activity)?
  3. If Keystroke is the correct approach, how do I ensure focus lands on the filter input inside the DDM popup after clicking the DDM?
